timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jean escapes bathroom via window, crashes through shower curtain, and falls down stairs while Grimsrud pursues; Carl exits through window, Grimsrud investigates.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl enters the Lundegaard house during Jean's escape and exits through the bathroom window to pursue her.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to kidnap Jean Lundegaard
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Outside the house after exiting through the bathroom window, continuing the pursuit.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Jean has not been captured; pursuit is ongoing.
evidence_quotes: As Carl enters,
Carl strides to the window and hoists himself out.

relation_updates: works with Grimsrud in home invasion

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Grimsrud discards the trooper's hat, praises Carl's smoothness, and tells him to clear the body; Carl exits the car as Jean screams briefly.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl witnesses Grimsrud shoot the trooper in the head and is ordered to clear the body from the road.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Clear the trooper's body from the road as instructed by Grimsrud.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Trooper is dead, and Carl exits the car to clear the body.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Carl must remove the trooper's body from the road.
evidence_quotes: GRIMSRUD Clear him off the road.
CARL Yeah. He gets out.
CARL (softly) Whoa... Whoa, Daddy.

relation_updates: Grimsrud assigns Carl cleanup duty
Carl refers to Grimsrud as 'Daddy'
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Carl refers to Grimsrud as 'Daddy'.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Marge, a police chief, receives earthworms from Norm, her husband, while discussing a lead on a tan Ciera linked to two men at the Blue Ox motel, where they had company.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl stops Grimsrud from retrieving Jean and laughs as she struggles hooded in the snow.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Grimsrud retrieves Jean after Carl's amused delay.
evidence_quotes: Grimsrud moves to retrieve her but Carl, grinning, lays a hand on his shoulder. CARL Hold it.
CARL Ha ha ha ha ha ha! Jesus!

relation_updates: asserts control over Grimsrud
demonstrates cruel amusement toward Jean
status_updates: arrives at lakeside cabin with victim
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Carl demonstrates cruel control and amusement during the kidnapping.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carl, bruised and enraged, demands Jerry deliver money to a parking ramp in 30 minutes, threatening to kill Jerry and his family if he fails.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: beaten kidnapper making ransom call with death threats
timeline_nodes.salient_development: After being beaten by Shep Proudfoot, Carl calls Jerry Lundegaard and demands ransom money, threatening to kill Jerry and his family if not delivered in 30 minutes.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: force Jerry to deliver ransom money immediately
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Jerry is given a 30-minute deadline to deliver money to the Dayton-Radisson parking ramp top level
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Jerry will comply with the ransom demand
evidence_quotes: Shep slaps him hard, forehand, backhand.
CARL All right, Jerry, I'm through fucking around. You got the fucking money?

relation_updates: assaulted by Shep Proudfoot
issues death threats to Jerry Lundegaard and family
auxiliary.persona_anchor: aggressive and injured kidnapper

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Wade and Carl meet on a snowy rooftop, exchange threats over money and Jean, shoot each other, and Carl flees with the briefcase after fatally wounding Wade.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl shoots Wade in the gut, is shot in the jaw by Wade, and after fatally shooting Wade, takes the money briefcase.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: acquire ransom money
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Fleeing in his car with the ransom money, with a severe jaw injury bleeding.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Carl's jaw injury and the aftermath of Wade's death.
evidence_quotes: He pulls out a gun and fires into Wade's gut.
His jaw is gouting blood.
He pockets the gun, picks up the briefcase one-handed, flings it into his car, gets in, peels out.

relation_updates: kills Wade during confrontation

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jerry discusses a $750,000 real estate deal with Wade, seeking approval for a parking lot on 40 acres, while Wade questions the investment and process.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl buries the ransom briefcase at a fence post in the snow and marks the spot with sticks.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Secure the ransom money by hiding it
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Briefcase buried at a fence post with sticks placed as a marker in the snow
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether the stick marker will allow successful retrieval among identical fence posts
evidence_quotes: He kneels at one of the fence posts and frantically digs into the snow with his bare hands, throws in the briefcase and covers it back up.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: Acts frantically and desperately, digging with bare hands and hurriedly marking the spot

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jerry discusses a $750,000 real estate deal with Wade, seeking approval for a parking lot on 40 acres, while Wade questions the investment and process.

timeline_nodes.resulting_state: has split the money and announced his departure with the Sierra

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jerry discusses a $750,000 real estate deal with Wade, seeking approval for a parking lot on 40 acres, while Wade questions the investment and process.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl argues with Grimsrud over car ownership, pulls a gun but pockets it, and is killed by Grimsrud with an ax.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: dead

Carl pulls out a gun.
Disgusted, Carl pockets the gun and heads for the door.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jerry discusses a $750,000 real estate deal with Wade, seeking approval for a parking lot on 40 acres, while Wade questions the investment and process.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: kidnapper negotiating terms with Jerry Lundegaard
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl meets Jerry, demands upfront payment of a car and $40,000, and after discussion, agrees to kidnap Jerry's wife for ransom.

timeline_nodes.resulting_state: agreed to proceed with the kidnapping, with Carl insisting on upfront terms
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: carrying out the kidnapping

The new vehicle, plus forty thousand dollars.
You want your own wife kidnapped?

client relationship with Jerry Lundegaard
auxiliary.persona_anchor: direct and skeptical in the negotiation

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Carl Showalter partners with Gaear Grimsrud to kidnap Jean Lundegaard for ransom from Jerry Lundegaard. During the crime, he becomes an accomplice to murder after a traffic stop, displays cruelty towards the victim, and escalates threats to secure payment. After a violent exchange where he kills Wade and obtains the money, he hides and splits the ransom, but is killed by Grimsrud in a dispute over a car.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jean escapes bathroom via window, crashes through shower curtain, and falls down stairs while Grimsrud pursues; Carl exits through window, Grimsrud investigates.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: car salesman in a customer dispute over TruCoat charge
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jerry is publicly accused of lying by a customer and reduces the TruCoat charge by $100 after the customer's protest.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to sell the car with the original TruCoat charge
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: customer pays the reduced price after accusing Jerry of lying
evidence_quotes: JERRY Well, he never done this before, but seein' as it's special circumstances and all, he says I can knock one hunnert off that TruCoat.
CUSTOMER Oh, for Christ's sake, where's my goddamn checkbook. Let's get this over with.

relation_updates: customer relationship damaged by accusation of dishonesty
status_updates: forced to reduce TruCoat charge by $100
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Jerry is accused of dishonest sales tactics and concedes under pressure

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jerry calls Wade but gets no answer; the scene shifts to a roadside Paul Bunyan statue at night as a car passes on an empty highway.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jerry's request for a $750,000 loan is rejected when Wade and Stan clarify they thought he was offering an investment, not seeking financing, and Wade threatens to proceed without him.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: secure $750,000 loan for parking lot deal
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Jerry vents frustration by scraping ice off his car windshield in a frenzy after the meeting.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how to finance the parking lot deal without Wade and Stan's loan
evidence_quotes: What kind of finder's fee were you looking for?
Jerry - we thought you were bringin' us an investment.
I need the principal.
Jerry goes into a frenzy, banging the scraper against the windshield and the hood of his car.

relation_updates: Wade and Stan reject Jerry's loan request
Wade threatens to proceed without Jerry
status_updates: becomes vulnerable to being excluded from his own deal
auxiliary.persona_anchor: shows Jerry's miscommunication and financial dependence on others

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jerry calls Wade but gets no answer; the scene shifts to a roadside Paul Bunyan statue at night as a car passes on an empty highway.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jerry finds his home disturbed with a shower curtain on the floor and an open bathroom window, then urgently calls Wade saying something terrible has happened.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: contact Wade about the disturbance
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: alarmed and waiting after attempting to call Wade
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: what happened at home and where is Jean
evidence_quotes: He has already seen the shower curtain on the floor.
He frowns, pokes at it with his foot.
JERRY ... Wade, it's Jerry, I - we gotta talk, Wade, it's terrible...
He looks at the open window, through which snow still sifts in.

relation_updates: attempts urgent contact with Wade about crisis
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Jerry shows concern and urgency upon discovering the disturbance and his wife's absence

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jerry comforts Scotty, assuring him they'll get Jean back by following the captors' demands without involving police, while instructing Scotty to lie if asked about her whereabouts.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jerry insists on no police involvement in the kidnapping and takes control of the ransom process, overriding Wade's objections with Stan's support.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: secure Jean's release by following kidnappers' instructions without police
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Jerry is set to prepare the ransom money for the drop, with Stan backing his approach.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether paying the ransom will secure Jean's release

I gotta tell ya, Wade, I'm leanin' to Jerry's viewpoint here.
They're gonna call, give me instructions for a drop. I'm supposed to have the money ready tomorrow.

gains Stan's support against Wade's objections

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carl, bruised and enraged, demands Jerry deliver money to a parking ramp in 30 minutes, threatening to kill Jerry and his family if he fails.

timeline_nodes.goal_state: secure the full ransom amount and address GMAC's legal threat
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: frustrated and under pressure from kidnappers and GMAC
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how to obtain the ransom money and avoid GMAC's legal consequences

The price is now the whole amount. We want the entire eighty thousand.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carl, bruised and enraged, demands Jerry deliver money to a parking ramp in 30 minutes, threatening to kill Jerry and his family if he fails.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Executive Sales Manager questioned by police about stolen vehicles

timeline_nodes.goal_state: avoid admitting involvement in vehicle thefts to police
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: immediately tries to contact Shep, showing increased urgency after police questioning
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Marge Gunderson will follow up on his denial and uncover the truth

auxiliary.persona_anchor: demonstrates deceptive behavior under police pressure and panic in covering tracks

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jerry drives the Cadillac past an empty exit booth with a prostrate body inside, the broken gate suggesting a hasty escape after the earlier shooting incident.

timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Leaves the rooftop with Wade's body hidden in the Cadillac's trunk

He wrestles Wade's body into the trunk, slams it shut

status_updates: takes possession of Wade's car and body

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Jerry Lundegaard, a dishonest car salesman, orchestrates his wife's kidnapping to extort ransom from his wealthy father-in-law to cover financial schemes. As the plan unravels, he faces loan audits, police investigation, and violent threats from kidnappers, leading him to cover up crimes and flee before being captured.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jerry comforts Scotty, assuring him they'll get Jean back by following the captors' demands without involving police, while instructing Scotty to lie if asked about her whereabouts.

timeline_nodes.goal_state: Identify the tan Ciera from the citation
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Lou acknowledges the correction, and the search is adjusted to focus on dealer plates
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Locating the specific tan Ciera with dealer plates

timeline_nodes.goal_state: To investigate the lead to the Twin Cities based on witness information
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Has gathered new witness statements about suspects' appearance and destination

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Marge Gunderson, a pregnant Brainerd police chief, investigates a triple homicide, methodically following leads from Minnesota to Minneapolis. She demonstrates sharp investigative instincts by correcting her partner's analysis and persistently pursuing deceptive suspects. After apprehending one killer and resolving the case, she returns to her supportive domestic life with her husband Norm.
